ops.body: Review finding in your in-flight didyoumean.go (uncommitted MCP-parity WIP): argSynonyms maps "tags" to {"label"}, but every tool schema accepts "labels" (tools_task.go, tools_planning.go, tools_runbook.go) — so the 'did you mean' hint never fires for tags. One-word fix: target "labels". Suggested regression test (verified: fails on "label", passes on "labels"): in package mcpserver, build a toolset via mcp.NewServer + all eight register*(ts, &bridge{}) calls, union all props.accepted, then assert every argSynonyms target is in that set. I had drafted this as didyoumean_test.go + the fix but reverted both rather than mutate your uncommitted file — session 48557191.
